en.extract:
Temminck's courser (Cursorius temminckii) is a bird in the pratincole and courser family, Glareolidae. It is a wader which lives in sub-Saharan Africa. It is noted for laying its dark ash-black eggs in the burnt bushes and grass of the African savannah.


Subspecies
There are three subspecies of Temminck's courser:

C. t. temminckii, (Swainson, 1822): Senegal to Ethiopia, Kenya and northern Tanzania
C. t. ruvanensis, (Madarász, 1915): southern Tanzania to Angola, Mozambique & northeast South Africa
C. t. aridus, (Clancey, 1989): northern Namibia to western Zimbabwe

This bird's common name and Latin binomial commemorate the Dutch naturalist Coenraad Jacob Temminck.


References

Temminck's courser - Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds.

'''Temminck's courser''' ('''''Cursorius temminckii''''') is a bird in the [[pratincole]] and [[courser]] family, Glareolidae. It is a [[wader]] which lives in [[sub-Saharan Africa]]. It is noted for laying its dark ash-black eggs in the burnt bushes and grass of the [[Africa]]n [[savannah]].<ref name="GoldammerRonde2004">{{cite book|author1=Johann Georg Goldammer|author2=Cornelis De Ronde|title=Wildland Fire Management Handbook for Sub-Sahara Africa|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=WmrvZqAB2UEC&pg=PA82|year=2004|publisher=African Minds|isbn=978-1-919833-65-1|pages=82–}}</ref><ref name="Frost1984">{{cite book|last1=Frost|first1=P. G. H.|title=Ecological Effects of Fire in South African Ecosystems|chapter=The Responses and Survival of Organisms in Fire-Prone Environments|series=Ecological Studies|volume=48|year=1984|pages=273–309|issn=0070-8356|doi=10.1007/978-3-642-69805-7_13|isbn=978-3-642-69807-1}}</ref><ref name="Engelbrecht2001">{{cite journal|last1=Engelbrecht|first1=G.D.|title=First record of the fledging period of Temminck's Courser, Cursorius temminckii|journal=Ostrich|volume=72|issue=1–2|year=2001|pages=117–118|issn=0030-6525|doi=10.2989/00306520109485296|bibcode=2001Ostri..72..117E |s2cid=88119355}}</ref>

==Subspecies==

There are three [[subspecies]] of Temminck's courser:<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | year=2019 | title=Sandpipers, snipes, coursers | work=World Bird List Version 9.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/sandpipers/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=25 June 2019 }}</ref>

*''C. t. temminckii'', <small>([[William Swainson|Swainson]], 1822)</small>: [[Senegal]] to [[Ethiopia]], [[Kenya]] and northern [[Tanzania]] 
*''C. t. ruvanensis'', <small>([[Gyula Madarász|Madarász]], 1915)</small>: southern [[Tanzania]] to [[Angola]], [[Mozambique]] & northeast [[South Africa]]
*''C. t. aridus'', <small>([[Phillip Clancey|Clancey]], 1989)</small>: northern [[Namibia]] to western [[Zimbabwe]]

[[File:Cursorius temmincki with juveniles.JPG|200px|thumbnail|left]]
This bird's common name and Latin binomial commemorate the Dutch naturalist [[Coenraad Jacob Temminck]].<ref>{{cite book|last=Beolens|first=Bo|title=Whose Bird? Men and Women Commemorated in the Common Names of Birds|year=2003|publisher=Christopher Helm|location=London|pages=335–336|author2=Watkins, Michael }}</ref>
